tagline: Reveal what generated code tries to hide.

cli.description: Local-first security sentinel for AI-generated code.

options.lang: output language: en, pt-BR, or es
options.governed: enable optional Sema governance companion checks
options.contractCheck: also report optional Sema governance gaps
options.requireContract: strict mode: block fixes without an applicable .sema contract

commands.semaSync: sync findings as local Sema events

dashboard.moreFindings: + {count} more findings. Run ward findings for the full list.

watch.started: The ward is watching your project.
watch.noNewIssues: No new high or critical issues found.

console.readyBody: Type /help to see commands. Press Ctrl+C or type /quit to exit.
console.scanHint: Run /scan to scan now while watch mode keeps listening.
console.historyHint: Use arrow keys to move through command history.
console.helpScan: /scan [path]      run a scan and keep this console open
console.helpStatus: /status           show the latest stored security summary
console.unknown: Unknown command. Use /help; commands start with /.
console.missingFindingId: Pass a finding id, for example /explain SW-AUTH-014.

status.noScan: No stored scan found. Run ward scan . first.

fix.title: SentryWard Fix Plan
fix.noAutoApply: This finding is not safe to auto-apply. Review the plan or create a patch.
fix.patchPlaceholder: Patch placeholder. This fix requires project-specific editing.
fix.plan.addAudit: Add an audit log for the sensitive action.
fix.plan.moveSecretBackend: Move privileged secret usage to backend environment variables.
fix.plan.rotateSecret: Warn that the exposed key must be rotated.
fix.plan.parameterizeQuery: Replace string-built SQL with parameterized queries.
fix.plan.addOwnership: Add tenant or user ownership checks.

hooks.noGit: This is not a Git repository.

surface.remoteBlocked: Only local development URLs are allowed.

evidence.noContract: Sensitive file has no applicable .sema contract: {file}

sema.reason.noContractRequired: No applicable .sema contract found and strict contract mode was requested.
sema.reason.noContractOptional: No applicable .sema contract found. Sema is an optional recommended complement.
sema.reason.blocked: The Sema gate blocked this fix.

constraint.preserveBehavior: Preserve existing behavior unless the contract allows a change.
constraint.updateContract: Update or validate the security contract after the patch when using Sema.
constraint.preservePublicRoutes: Do not change public route behavior.
constraint.addAudit: Add audit logging for sensitive admin actions.
constraint.noSecretReturn: Do not return or log secrets.
constraint.noRawSql: Do not introduce unsafe raw SQL.
constraint.createContractFirst: Create or update the applicable .sema contract before changing code.
constraint.recommendedComplement: Sema can be added as an optional governance complement.

evidence.cookieRouteNoCsrf: Cookie or session route has no visible CSRF protection.
evidence.sensitiveRouteNoRateLimit: Sensitive route has no visible rate limit.
evidence.webhookNoSignature: Webhook endpoint has no visible signature verification.

default.title: Security issue detected ({id})
default.problem: A local deterministic rule found a risky security pattern.
default.impact: The issue may expose data, credentials, or protected behavior.
default.recommendation: Review the affected file and apply the smallest safe fix.

SW-SECRETS-001.title: Stripe secret key exposed
SW-SECRETS-001.problem: A Stripe secret key appears in local code or configuration.
SW-SECRETS-001.impact: Anyone who obtains this key may perform privileged Stripe API actions.
SW-SECRETS-001.recommendation: Move the key to backend-only environment variables and rotate it.

SW-SECRETS-002.title: OpenAI API key exposed
SW-SECRETS-002.problem: An OpenAI API key appears in local code or configuration.
SW-SECRETS-002.impact: The key can be abused to spend credits or access project resources.
SW-SECRETS-002.recommendation: Remove the key from code, use environment variables, and rotate it.

SW-SECRETS-003.title: Supabase service role key exposed
SW-SECRETS-003.problem: A Supabase service role credential appears in code.
SW-SECRETS-003.impact: Service role keys can bypass Row Level Security.
SW-SECRETS-003.recommendation: Keep service role keys server-only and rotate the exposed key.

SW-BROWSER-001.title: Secret exposed in frontend code
SW-BROWSER-001.problem: A private-looking secret appears in code likely shipped to the browser.
SW-BROWSER-001.impact: Attackers can inspect browser bundles and extract the value.
SW-BROWSER-001.recommendation: Move privileged logic to the backend and expose only public client-safe keys.

SW-BROWSER-002.title: Sensitive route referenced in frontend code
SW-BROWSER-002.problem: Frontend code references a sensitive route.
SW-BROWSER-002.impact: Visibility is not automatically a vulnerability, but it raises risk if backend auth is weak.
SW-BROWSER-002.recommendation: Verify backend-side authentication, role checks, ownership checks, and rate limits.

SW-AUTH-014.title: Admin route without authentication
SW-AUTH-014.problem: A sensitive route appears to lack backend authentication or role validation.
SW-AUTH-014.impact: Anyone may be able to call an administrative endpoint.
SW-AUTH-014.recommendation: Use existing auth middleware, validate admin role, and preserve route behavior.

SW-DB-001.title: SQL interpolation risk
SW-DB-001.problem: SQL appears to be built with template interpolation.
SW-DB-001.impact: Untrusted input may alter query behavior.
SW-DB-001.recommendation: Use parameterized queries or safe ORM APIs.

SW-UPLOAD-001.title: Upload without visible size limit
SW-UPLOAD-001.problem: File upload handling has no visible size limit.
SW-UPLOAD-001.impact: Large uploads can exhaust disk, memory, or processing resources.
SW-UPLOAD-001.recommendation: Add explicit size limits and reject oversized files early.

SW-CORS-001.title: Wildcard CORS origin
SW-CORS-001.impact: Browsers may allow unintended sites to call the API.
SW-CORS-001.recommendation: Restrict origins to trusted domains, especially in production.

SW-SESSION-001.title: JWT stored in browser storage
SW-SESSION-001.problem: A token is stored in localStorage or sessionStorage.
SW-SESSION-001.recommendation: Use secure HttpOnly cookies or another safer session design.

SW-WEBHOOK-001.title: Webhook without signature verification
SW-WEBHOOK-001.problem: A webhook endpoint has no visible signature verification.
SW-WEBHOOK-001.recommendation: Verify provider signatures before trusting the payload.

SW-DOCKER-002.title: Docker image copies .env
SW-DOCKER-002.problem: The Dockerfile copies a .env file into the image.
SW-DOCKER-002.impact: Secrets can be baked into images and distributed.
SW-DOCKER-002.recommendation: Inject secrets at runtime and keep .env out of images.

SW-CI-001.title: pull_request_target workflow risk
SW-CI-001.impact: Untrusted pull requests can access privileged workflow context if mishandled.
SW-CI-001.recommendation: Use pull_request where possible and avoid checking out untrusted code with secrets.

SW-PROMPT-001.title: Untrusted content mixed into high-priority prompt
SW-PROMPT-001.problem: User or document content appears inside a system or developer prompt.
SW-PROMPT-001.impact: Prompt injection can override intended instructions.
SW-PROMPT-001.recommendation: Keep untrusted content in user context and separate it from system instructions.

SW-FIREBASE-001.title: Firebase service account exposed
SW-FIREBASE-001.problem: A Firebase service account private key appears in code.
SW-FIREBASE-001.impact: The credential can grant privileged Firebase access.
SW-FIREBASE-001.recommendation: Remove the service account, rotate it, and load it from a secure server-side secret store.

SW-SEMA-001.title: Sensitive code without Sema contract coverage
SW-SEMA-001.problem: Sensitive code has no applicable .sema contract.
SW-SEMA-001.impact: AI-assisted fixes may change protected behavior without semantic governance.
SW-SEMA-001.recommendation: If the project wants Sema governance, create or link an applicable .sema contract.
